mnemonics: Remember the 4 D's of Data: Description, Diagnosis, Prediction, Prescription
keyPoints: Data is the foundation of any machine learning project, consisting of raw facts and figures.
Understanding the type of data (e.g., numerical, categorical, time-series) is crucial for choosing the right algorithms.
The quality and quantity of data directly impact the performance and accuracy of a machine learning model.
commonMistakes: Assuming more data is always better, without considering its quality, relevance, or potential for bias.
Treating all data types the same way, leading to inappropriate preprocessing and model selection.
Focusing solely on complex algorithms while neglecting the fundamental step of thoroughly exploring and understanding the data first.
